purpose: Seed prompts for evaluating whether each academic research skill should trigger. These are static boundary fixtures, not a substitute for runtime agent trigger telemetry.

should_trigger: Choose and configure MCP servers for arXiv, OpenAlex, Semantic Scholar, Zotero, and Overleaf in this research repo.
Audit whether our scholarly search MCP setup can support citation graph and author lookup workflows.
Prepare the literature workflow stack so a SOTA agent can use arXiv, DBLP, Semantic Scholar, and OpenAlex without manual MCP stitching.
should_not_trigger: Summarize this already downloaded PDF without changing any MCP configuration.

should_trigger: Review this draft like a severe but fair top-tier conference reviewer before submission.
Find the strongest novelty, methodology, and related-work objections reviewers could raise.
should_not_trigger: Format the bibliography keys in this BibTeX file.

should_trigger: Prepare the code, data, commands, and metadata for an artifact evaluation submission.
Audit this project for open science release readiness, including reproducibility and dataset card gaps.
Map this research project to ACM artifact badges, including Available, Functional, Reusable, Reproduced, and Replicated.
should_not_trigger: Brainstorm three possible research questions for a new idea.

should_trigger: Audit whether this paper can support ACM artifact badges, open-science badges, TOP transparency, and venue compliance profiles.
Update badge evidence rows for this contribution, paper release, and submission checklist.
should_not_trigger: Draft the introduction section from an accepted paper frame.

should_trigger: Normalize this BibTeX file, citation keys, DOI metadata, and unused references.
Check the bibliography for duplicate entries, missing identifiers, and inconsistent arXiv or DOI fields.
should_not_trigger: Judge whether the paper's central claim is actually supported by the cited evidence.

should_trigger: Verify every factual claim in this related work section against the cited papers.
Audit whether these numerical results and citation statements are supported by the source PDFs.
should_not_trigger: Convert the paper PDF to Markdown for later reading.

should_trigger: Create a contribution package from this accepted research agenda opportunity with a manifest, claim map, badge plan, outputs, report, and paper export.
Organize this artifact, experiment, analysis, and software contribution under one contribution ID.
should_not_trigger: Do a quick one-off exploratory plot without promoting it to a contribution.

should_trigger: Design a CS evaluation plan with baselines, metrics, ablations, datasets, and validity threats.
Audit whether this experiment section is convincing for a systems or ML conference.
should_not_trigger: Pick the best conference deadline for this already finished paper.

should_trigger: Compare possible CS venues for this paper and explain fit, reviewer expectations, and artifact requirements.
Help position this contribution for a conference track with the right deadline and framing.
should_not_trigger: Run the benchmark script and save the experiment log.

should_trigger: Convert these PDFs and DOCX proposal files into Markdown with table and figure extraction notes.
Extract text, tables, and assets from scanned papers and report conversion quality.
should_not_trigger: Decide which arXiv papers should be included in the SOTA review.

should_trigger: Assess privacy, consent, scraping, license, and retention risks for this research dataset.
Document IRB, sensitive content, public web data, and sharing constraints before release.
should_not_trigger: Polish the abstract for a stronger submission narrative.

should_trigger: Plan and record an ablation run with config, command, seed, metric, and outcome notes.
Compare these overnight training runs and update the experiment logbook.
Set up an autonomous experiment campaign with a frozen harness, frontier ledger, and keep/discard/crash policy.
should_not_trigger: Move a messy downloaded source archive into the repo structure.

should_trigger: Revise this manuscript introduction, methods, limitations, and related work for academic submission quality.
Draft a clear paper section without inventing citations or overstating evidence.
should_not_trigger: Install MCP servers for Semantic Scholar and OpenAlex.

should_trigger: Choose a venue-aware paper frame from these reviewed contribution packages and evidence maps.
Build the argument map, evidence map, badge fit, and release implications before manuscript writing.
should_not_trigger: Prepare checksums and staged files for an already accepted release plan.

should_trigger: Create a paper-specific release manifest, source map, lock, checksums, metadata, and staged artifact package.
Audit whether this paper release package has stale copied files or missing checksum evidence.
should_not_trigger: Pick the best venue framing for these contributions.

should_trigger: Prepare the cover letter, submission checklist, submitted version lock, venue-system notes, and review-round folders for this paper.
Track the decision letter, correspondence, camera-ready state, and submitted files for this manuscript.
should_not_trigger: Run a new experiment requested by a reviewer.

should_trigger: Prepare publication-facing figures, generated table fragments, captions, source data, and asset-map entries.
Audit whether these paper figures and tables are stale or inconsistent with the analysis outputs.
should_not_trigger: Write the response letter to reviewer comments.

should_trigger: Map reviewer comments into concede, defend, reframe, and revise actions for a rebuttal.
Write a precise response plan and camera-ready revision checklist from these reviews.
should_not_trigger: Create an initial SOTA matrix from a folder of PDFs.

should_trigger: Migrate this messy academic repo, proposal PDFs, notebooks, and archives into the standard research structure.
Unpack downloaded archives and reorganize them into sources, experiments, papers, and docs without losing provenance.
should_not_trigger: Choose statistical tests for a cleaned results table.

should_trigger: Clean this tabular dataset, run the analysis, and produce publication-ready figures and result tables.
Move notebook analysis into reproducible code and document the statistical choices.
should_not_trigger: Review the manuscript as an adversarial peer reviewer.

should_trigger: Turn these SOTA gaps and survey claims into prioritized feasible research opportunities.
Decide which research directions are publishable enough to become contribution packages.
should_not_trigger: Normalize this BibTeX file.

should_trigger: Write the analysis report from completed generated tables, figures, limitations, and negative results.
Summarize result interpretation while linking directly to output data and paper-export assets.
should_not_trigger: Select a target conference track.

should_trigger: Turn this rough research idea into questions, hypotheses, contribution claims, and evidence requirements.
Identify the novelty gap, scope boundaries, and publishable positioning before we start experiments.
should_not_trigger: Normalize the BibTeX citation keys.

should_trigger: Audit and repair the research repo structure, wiki, logs, sources, outputs, and reproducibility docs.
Keep the project documentation, capability profile, and research records coherent after recent changes.
should_not_trigger: Write a detailed rebuttal to reviewer 2.

should_trigger: This academic repo task spans sources, SOTA, citation audit, experiments, and paper writing; choose the right workflow.
I am not sure which research skill applies to this project maintenance and literature review request.
should_not_trigger: Use the source-ingestion skill to register this one PDF.

should_trigger: Inspect this external paper code repository and reproduce the documented evaluation or inference command.
Audit whether the README, datasets, checkpoints, and scripts support the paper's reproducibility claims.
should_not_trigger: Design a new research question for our own project.

should_trigger: Build a research dashboard for experiment metrics, source review, or annotation workflows.
Prototype a data browser that supports the paper's analysis and lets users inspect evidence.
should_not_trigger: Convert a scanned PDF into Markdown.

should_trigger: Pressure-test this academic research skill package before publishing it.
Review whether these skills have good trigger descriptions, references, install behavior, and validation.
should_not_trigger: Run an ablation experiment and log the result.

should_trigger: Build a SOTA matrix from these papers and synthesize gaps, trends, and related-work claims.
Compare the literature around this topic and identify what is novel enough for a contribution.
From this rough idea and two seed papers, run a full-survey SOTA with forward and backward citation chasing, full-text paper syntheses, a clean references.bib, and a LaTeX survey draft.
should_not_trigger: Prepare artifact evaluation packaging for an accepted paper.

should_trigger: Add these PDFs, arXiv links, Zotero items, and proposal files to the research source ledger.
Register a new source with provenance, metadata, Markdown notes, and wiki links.
should_not_trigger: Choose between two conference venues.

should_trigger: Turn reviewed SOTA claims into a section-by-section survey with survey claim ledger rows.
Plan, draft, and review a survey explaining research threads, methodologies, experiments, gaps, and contradictions.
should_not_trigger: Run benchmark experiments for a contribution package.

should_trigger: Plan and document a PRISMA-style systematic review with inclusion criteria and screening decisions.
Run a reproducible search strategy and create flow counts for included and excluded papers.
should_not_trigger: Do a quick informal related-work summary without systematic screening.
